{"step_by_step_thinking": "The definition of pyrexia of unknown origin (FUO) typically includes a fever that persists for at least 3 weeks despite a thorough workup. Option A aligns with this definition. Option B states that the fever is undiagnosed after 1 week of in-patient workup, which is a shorter timeframe than the typical definition. Option C mentions absence of immunological compromise, which is not a defining characteristic of FUO. Option D specifies a temperature of 38.3°C or more, which is a common threshold for fever.
